title: L6: Turing's Mug
description: Applying Year 8 data representation ideas in a mixed challenge lesson.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

Quick check from Large Quantities.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-dna',questions:[{"prompt":"How many bits are in a byte?","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 2 KB to bytes.","answer":"2000","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = low/high voltage, B = a lesson title, C = a paragraph heading. Which can physically represent bits?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 16 bits to bytes.","answer":"2","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Challenge Briefing
steps.content:
Turing's mug

This final lesson is a mixed revision lesson. Each question uses one skill from the unit. Do one step at a time: identify the type of question, then apply the rule you have already practised.

Question typeWhat to do
Representation choiceChoose the option that fits the task.
Symbol lengthCount the visible symbols.
4-bit binaryUse 8, 4, 2, 1.
UnitsUse 1 byte = 8 bits and 1 KB = 1000 bytes.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-briefing-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A = representation choice, B = symbol count, C = binary conversion. Which method uses 8, 4, 2, 1?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = representation choice, B = symbol count, C = binary conversion. Which method counts visible dots, dashes, 0s or 1s?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Revision Map
steps.content:
Revision map

This lesson is designed to take the whole hour as a mixed application lesson. Use the map below to choose the method before answering.

If the question mentions...Use this method
best representationIdentify the task, then choose the clearest option
symbols or charactersCount exactly what is shown
binary or 4-bitUse 8, 4, 2, 1
bits, bytes or KBUse the unit conversion fact in the question
physical representationChoose voltage, light pulse, charge or another real signal

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-map-check',questions:[{"prompt":"A = unit conversion, B = representation choice, C = physical representation. A question about bytes and bits uses which method?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = unit conversion, B = representation choice, C = physical representation. A question about voltage or light pulses uses which method?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Decode the Mug
steps.content:
Step through the mixed challenge

Click through each clue and connect it to the correct skill.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr6-stepper-1',steps:[{"title":"Clue 1: binary digits","html":BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,1,0,1],[8,4,2,1]),"note":"0101 is a sequence of four bits."},{"title":"Convert the number","html":"

4 + 1 = 5

","note":"The active columns are 4 and 1, so 0101 is 5 in denary."},{"title":"Clue 2: character symbols","html":"

... --- ...

","note":"This is Morse code. Counting dot and dash symbols gives 9 symbols."},{"title":"Clue 3: representation size","html":"

8 bits = 1 byte

","note":"Large quantities are described using units such as bits, nibbles, bytes, KB and MB."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-guided1-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Convert 0101 to denary.","answer":"5","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many dot/dash symbols are in ...---... ?","answer":"9","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Choose the Method
steps.content:
Step through method choice

The most important skill in the final lesson is choosing the right method before answering.

Check your understanding

Complete this short activity before moving on.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({completeOnEnd:false,containerId:'y8dr6-stepper-2',steps:[{"title":"Question: Convert 1010 to denary","html":"

Method: 4-bit binary

","note":"Use 8, 4, 2, 1."},{"title":"Apply the method","html":BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,1,0],[8,4,2,1]),"note":"8 + 2 = 10."},{"title":"Question: Convert 32 bits to bytes","html":"

Method: unit conversion

","note":"Use 1 byte = 8 bits."},{"title":"Apply the method","html":"

32 / 8 = 4 bytes

","note":"Bits to bytes means divide by 8."}]});BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-guided2-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Convert 1010 to denary.","answer":"10","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 32 bits to bytes.","answer":"4","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Mini Check: Pick the Method
steps.content:
Mini check: pick the method

Do not solve yet. Pick the method. Type A, B, C or D.

LetterMethod
AChoose a representation for a task
BCount symbols
CUse 8, 4, 2, 1
DConvert units
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-mini-check',questions:[{"prompt":"Question: How many bits are in 101100? Which method?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct: count symbols."},{"prompt":"Question: Convert 0110 to denary. Which method?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct: use 8, 4, 2, 1."},{"prompt":"Question: Convert 4 bytes to bits. Which method?","answers":["D","d"],"right":"Correct: convert units."},{"prompt":"Question: Which is best for showing a route? Which method?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct: choose a representation."}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 1
steps.content:
Checked practice: mixed calculations

Each question tells you which rule to use. Type only the answer.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-practice-a',questions:[{"prompt":"4-bit binary uses 8, 4, 2, 1. Convert 0111 to denary.","answer":"7","right":"Correct: 4 + 2 + 1 = 7."},{"prompt":"Count dot/dash symbols. How many symbols are in ---- ?","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. How many bytes are in 16 bits?","answer":"2","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"4-bit binary uses 8, 4, 2, 1. Convert 12 to binary.","answer":"1100","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 KB = 1000 bytes. Convert 2 KB to bytes.","answer":"2000","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 2
steps.content:
Checked practice: mixed choices

Use the labelled options. Type A, B or C.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-practice-b',questions:[{"prompt":"A = map, B = table, C = warning sound. Which is best for showing a route?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = Morse, B = nibble, C = byte. Which is a character coding scheme?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = voltage, B = table title, C = lesson name. Which can physically represent bits?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = keep, B = send, C = use. A calculator adds two numbers. Type A, B or C.","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = 4 bits, B = 8 bits, C = 1000 bytes. Which is one byte?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Extension: Full Mixed Challenge
steps.content:
Extension challenge

Fast finishers can complete this final mixed set. Each question includes enough information to answer it.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-extension',questions:[{"prompt":"4-bit binary uses 8, 4, 2, 1. Convert 1110 to denary.","answer":"14","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 56 bits to bytes.","answer":"7","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Count the dot/dash symbols in .--.","answer":"4","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = map, B = table, C = alarm. Which is best for warning people quickly?","answers":["C","c"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 6 to 4-bit binary.","answer":"0110","right":"Correct."}]});

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 generated mixed questions correctly to complete this step.
steps.js: (function(){var bank=[{"prompt":"Convert 1000 to denary.","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Convert 10 to 4-bit binary.","answer":"1010","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bits are in 00110011?","answer":"8","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"How many bytes are in 24 bits?","answer":"3","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = sign/alarm, B = table, C = binary number. Which is suitable for communicating a warning quickly?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = Morse, B = KB, C = voltage. Which is a character coding scheme?","answers":["A","a"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 KB = 1000 bytes. Convert 4 KB to bytes.","answer":"4000","right":"Correct."}];var qs=[];for(var i=0;i<5;i++){qs.push(bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)]);}BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-endless',questions:qs});})();

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
End of lesson exam

Final mixed check for the unit.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y8dr6-exam',questions:[{"prompt":"4-bit binary uses 8, 4, 2, 1. Convert 1100 to denary.","answer":"12","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"4-bit binary uses 8, 4, 2, 1. Convert 9 to binary.","answer":"1001","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"Count dot/dash symbols. How many symbols are in ...--- ?","answer":"6","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"1 byte = 8 bits. Convert 3 bytes to bits.","answer":"24","right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A = map, B = table, C = warning alarm. Which is best for organised rows and columns?","answers":["B","b"],"right":"Correct."},{"prompt":"A representation is a way of showing what?","answers":["information","data"],"right":"Correct."}]});

quizQuestions.q: Which is a character coding scheme?
Which option is best for showing a location?
Which purpose means using information to produce a decision or result?
Which could physically represent binary digits in a device?
